refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure update latency statistics (p95), validate data accuracy through cross-checks with sensor inputs, and confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with in-memory caching.
DP3: Event-driven notification system for model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to meet latency and accuracy requirements.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry ingestion and model update timing.
Dependency of notification system on model update completion.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces to minimize ripple effects.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency is stable and within acceptable bounds.
Risk of telemetry data loss or corruption impacting model accuracy.
Risk of processing bottlenecks under peak load conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor real-time performance and consider fallback mechanisms for telemetry loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry ingestion, model update, and distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from Vehicle and other sensors.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation, Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Teleoperation for task execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as core processing is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Limited containment; mostly data source, high diffusion if processing done here.
Receives updates but does not process core model updates.
Consumes safety alerts; no containment of update logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes latency and diffusion risks, ensuring timeliness and safety compliance.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure in NGHE Solution module.
Scalability challenges under high data volume.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement redundancy and load balancing within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly maps to selected DPs with measurable latency targets and logical solution concepts.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution.
Interfaces are well-defined and limited to known external interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support the architectural decision to assign update logic to NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements and interface monitoring at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership and module boundaries align with stakeholder responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback and redundancy mechanisms in NGHE Solution to mitigate single point of failure.
Monitor network link performance as a critical external factor.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency and safety requirements with clear ownership and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; next step is to decompose sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network link latency remains stable and within bounds.
NGHE Solution module can scale to required load.
open_risks: Single point of failure in NGHE Solution.
Potential telemetry data loss or corruption.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational load.
System resilience and failover test results.
